mitigationRefs.mitigation: Static analysis runs against the decision runtime and API handlers that enforce deny-by-default behavior.
Static analysis runs with the connector security gate so provider adapters keep explicit no-write and secret-handling boundaries.
Deployment-scoped dynamic testing must exercise protected API routes and fail closed when authentication or readiness evidence is missing.
DAST output or a deployment-scoped exception must be retained with the release instead of being left as chat-only or CI-only context.
Dependency audit findings are release-gated before vulnerable decision or API dependencies can be promoted.
Dependency scanning covers connector runtime libraries before live provider evidence depends on them.
Release artifacts carry SBOM and provenance metadata before deployment manifests can claim production persistence readiness.
Signed image and provenance records bind evidence claims to an immutable release digest.
Malformed graph fuzzing preserves deny-by-default and bounded traversal behavior under hostile policy inputs.
Policy fuzzing keeps tenant boundaries explicit when relationship graphs contain malformed or adversarial tuples.
Runtime and policy harness tests retain proof that cross-tenant relationship traversal, resource lookup, connector-state bleed, and evidence export leakage are denied.
API runtime abuse tests cover safe evidence export behavior for tenant-scoped requests.
Threat-model refreshes keep decision bypass, admin takeover, and explanation leakage mitigations current for each release.
Threat-model refreshes review live connector credential, consent, readback, and no-write boundaries before connector evidence is promoted.
Threat-model refreshes review audit, queue, graph, and evidence-store trust boundaries when release changes touch persistence.
High or critical findings that affect decision, admin, or API authorization must be triaged before release promotion.
Findings that affect audit durability, queue replay, or graph state integrity require retained triage before release promotion.
Release triage records must distinguish resolved findings, accepted proof-point gaps, and blocked production claims.
SSDF evidence maps release controls to Access Kit authorization safeguards without implying ATO approval.
SSDF mapping ties retained artifacts to control families and known production gaps for assessor review.
